title: Support with weighted averages using the formula column
excerpt: I am building a simple report for incoming apartment communities to our company, and I have included a formula column to divide the deal value by the number of units for each property so we can see what our fee per door …

posts.author: VGJames (James Sellar)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am building a simple report for incoming apartment communities to our company, and I have included a formula column to divide the deal value by the number of units for each property so we can see what our fee per door is. However, I need what is currently showing as N/A to display the weighted average. I know this is not possible as that ‘cell’ is only tied to the formula column.

Does anyone know of a workaround? I would like to show the total weighted average in the table, but if it has to be a widget, I’ll take that as a plan B.

All I want to do is divide $1,431,253 by 2072!

Please help!

Monday Screenshot418×428 26.7 KB

posts.author: trevor13 (Trevor Chauvin-DeCaro)
posts.cookedHtml:
James Sellar:

I am building a simple report for incoming apartment communities to our company, and I have included a formula column to divide the deal value by the number of units for each property so we can see what our fee per door is. However, I need what is currently showing as N/A to display the weighted average. I know this is not possible as that ‘cell’ is only tied to the formula column.

Does anyone know of a workaround? I would like to show the total weighted average in the table, but if it has to be a widget, I’ll take that as a plan B.

All I want to do is divide $1,431,253 by 2072!

Please help!

Let’s give these workarounds a try!

Option 1: Add a Weighted Average Column

  1. Add a Number column called Weighted $/Door.
  2. Use a Formula:

CopyEdit

{Deal Value} / {Units}
  1. Duplicate this formula in a Mirror column or convert to a Numbers column using an Automation + Integromat/Make if you want it to sum/average in the footer.

Option 2: Dashboard Widget (Plan B)

  • Use a Numbers Widget or Chart Widget on a dashboard:
    • Set it to sum Deal Value and sum Units.
    • Create a formula on the widget:

mathematica

CopyEdit

Total Deal Value ÷ Total Units
  • This gives you the weighted average visually without messing with the board layout.

Option 3: One-Line Formula for Weighted Average (Board-Level)

  • Add a Formula Column at the bottom for just one row to calculate:

pgsql

CopyEdit

SUM({Deal Value}) / SUM({Units})

Limitation: Monday formulas can’t do cross-item aggregation, so you’d need a Rollup in a Parent Group or Dashboard for this to work.

Since your exact goal is:

“All I want to do is divide $1,431,253 by 2,072!”

Use a Dashboard Formula Widget to calculate:

mathematica

CopyEdit

{Sum of Deal Value} ÷ {Sum of Units}

This is the most reliable way to display a weighted average for the whole board.
